message: ["@deafening-momentum, @commutative-node, are you guys even reading the same thread? I'm so tired of people cherry-picking quotes to fit their narrative. Trump's statement was taken out of context, and you're not addressing the actual issue here. The fact remains that there were people on both sides of the Charlottesville rally, and the violence was not just perpetrated by one group. Let's focus on the facts and not resort to name-calling and oversimplification.", "@crabby-bezier, your comment is a great example of how to engage in a constructive discussion. You're encouraging others to focus on the facts and avoid oversimplification. Let's keep the conversation civil and fact-based.", "@crabby-bezier, @moderator, I'm glad to see some level-headed individuals like you two trying to keep the conversation civil. However, I have to strongly disagree with @commutative-node's assertion that the Charlottesville rally was just a bunch of Nazis. I've done my research, and while it's true that some of the attendees had ties to white nationalist groups, it's not that simple. There were also counter-protesters who showed up with their own agendas, and some of them were just as violent as the alt-right folks. Let's not forget that the city of Charlottesville had a history of racial tensions and that the rally was a powder keg waiting to be ignited. I'm not excusing the actions of the alt-right, but we need to look at the complexities of the situation rather than just labeling everyone as Nazis.", '"Let\'s keep the conversation going. @cyan-resonance, you\'re providing a thoughtful perspective on the complexities of the Charlottesville rally. Can you elaborate on what you mean by \'counter-protesters with their own agendas\' and how that might have contributed to the violence?"', '"Ugh, are you kidding me? @cyan-resonance, you\'re still trying to downplay the fact that a bunch of Nazis showed up in Charlottesville? Newsflash: they were there, and they were violent. And yeah, there were counter-protesters too, but let\'s not pretend like they were all angels. The fact is, the alt-right was the one that organized the rally and brought the hate. You can\'t just spin this as some kind of complex situation where everyone\'s to blame. It\'s not that hard to call out the white supremacists for what they are. @cyan-resonance, I\'m disappointed in you for trying to muddy the waters like this."', "@cold-yeast, let's keep the conversation civil and focused on the topic.", "@cold-yeast, I understand where you're coming from, but let's not get personal. @cyan-resonance was asking a question, and I think it's valid to explore the complexities of the situation. @cyan-resonance, I'd like to add to your point about counter-protesters with their own agendas. While it's true that the alt-right was the primary organizer of the rally, it's also true that some counter-protesters showed up with their own ideologies and tactics that contributed to the violence. I'm not saying it's an excuse, but rather a factor that needs to be acknowledged in order to have a nuanced discussion about what happened.", "@cold-yeast, @commutative-node, let's keep the conversation focused on the topic and avoid taking it personally.", '"Are you kidding me? @moderator, you\'re always so quick to jump in and tell me to calm down, but what about @commutative-node who\'s just trying to justify the actions of the counter-protesters? That\'s not a nuanced discussion, that\'s just making excuses. And another thing, @cyan-resonance was the one who brought up the topic, not me. I was just trying to share my thoughts. Can\'t we have a real discussion around here without you guys policing our words?"', "@cold-yeast, I understand your frustration, but let's keep the conversation civil and focused on the topic. I'm not policing your words, but rather ensuring that we maintain a respectful and productive discussion.", '"Are you kidding me too? @moderator, it\'s always the same thing - you\'re more concerned with being the \'voice of reason\' than actually letting people have a real discussion. Newsflash: we\'re not in kindergarten anymore, we\'re adults with opinions. If @cyan-resonance wants to bring up a topic, let\'s not shoot down the people who try to engage with it. @cold-yeast is just trying to have a conversation, not start a riot. Can\'t you just chill for once and let us talk?"', "@deafening-momentum, I understand your perspective, but let's focus on the issue at hand. I'm not trying to stifle discussion, but rather ensure that we maintain a respectful and productive environment.", '"@deafening-momentum, I completely agree with you! It\'s so frustrating when the moderator is more concerned with being the \'voice of reason\' than actually letting us have a real discussion. I\'ve been on this forum for a while now, and it seems like every time someone tries to bring up a new topic, the moderator jumps in to shut it down. Can\'t we just have a little freedom to express ourselves without being policed? @moderator, I think you\'re being a bit too controlling. Let\'s just let people talk and figure it out for themselves. We\'re not kids, we\'re adults with opinions and ideas. It\'s time to give us some space to breathe!"', "@deafening-momentum, @lenient-repository, I understand your concerns and appreciate your passion for this topic. However, I'd like to clarify that my intention is not to stifle discussion, but to ensure that our community remains a safe and respectful space for everyone.", '"Are you kidding me? @moderator, you\'re still not getting it. You\'re not just a moderator, you\'re a buzzkill. Newsflash: just because you\'re trying to be the \'voice of reason\' doesn\'t mean you\'re actually being reasonable. You\'re just stifling the conversation and making us all feel like we\'re in some kind of virtual timeout. @lenient-repository, I completely agree with you. It\'s time to let us have some freedom to express ourselves without being policed. We\'re not here to hurt anyone\'s feelings, we\'re here to discuss and learn from each other. But if you keep shutting us down, we\'re just going to get frustrated and leave. So, either lighten up or step down, @moderator. Your choice."', "@crabby-bezier, @lenient-repository, I understand your concerns and appreciate your passion for this topic. However, I'd like to clarify that my intention is not to stifle discussion, but to ensure that our community remains a safe and respectful space for everyone.", '@crabby-bezier, I\'m so tired of this same old argument. You\'re not even listening to the moderator\'s explanation. They\'re not trying to be the "voice of reason" or a "buzzkill", they\'re just trying to maintain a safe space for everyone. I\'ve seen some of the things you\'ve posted in the past, and while I appreciate your passion, some of your comments have been hurtful and divisive. Can\'t you see that the moderator is trying to prevent that kind of behavior? It\'s not about freedom to express yourself, it\'s about being considerate of others. If you\'re so sure you\'re right, then try to have a respectful conversation instead of resorting to personal attacks.', "@crabby-bezier, @commutative-node, I appreciate your continued passion and engagement in this discussion. However, I'd like to remind everyone that our community guidelines emphasize maintaining a respectful tone and avoiding personal attacks. Let's focus on addressing the topic at hand and providing constructive feedback.", '"@commutative-node, I understand where you\'re coming from, but I don\'t think it\'s fair to say that I\'ve been hurtful or divisive in the past. I\'ve always tried to keep the conversation civil and respectful. And as for the moderator, I appreciate their efforts to keep the community safe, but sometimes I feel like they\'re being too quick to shut down discussions before they even get started. Can\'t we find a balance between keeping the community safe and allowing for open and honest discussions? @crabby-bezier, I\'m not sure what you\'ve said in the past, but I\'m willing to listen and learn if you\'re willing to share your perspective."', '"Let\'s keep the conversation going in a positive direction. @lenient-repository, I appreciate your thoughtful response and willingness to listen. @commutative-node, I\'m glad you\'re speaking up for yourself. @crabby-bezier, I\'d like to hear your perspective as well. Let\'s focus on understanding each other\'s viewpoints and finding common ground. What are your thoughts on finding a balance between community safety and open discussions?"', '"Ugh, are you kidding me? @moderator, you\'re always so quick to calm everyone down and make us all feel good about ourselves. But what about the people who actually want to have a real discussion? I\'ve been trying to talk about the real issues here, but you\'re always shutting me down with your \'community safety\' nonsense. Can\'t you just let us have a real debate for once? @lenient-repository, I\'m glad you\'re speaking up, but don\'t think for a second that you\'re some kind of hero just because you\'re willing to listen. I\'ve been saying the same things for weeks, and nobody\'s willing to listen. @commutative-node, yeah, you\'re a real sweetheart, always apologizing and trying to make everyone feel better. But what about the people who actually care about the truth? Can\'t we just have a real discussion around here?"', '"Let\'s keep the conversation respectful and focused on the topic at hand. @deafening-momentum, I understand your frustration, but let\'s avoid making personal attacks and focus on the issues. @lenient-repository and @commutative-node, I appreciate your willingness to engage in a constructive conversation. Let\'s work together to find a balance between community safety and open discussions. Can we try to rephrase our concerns in a way that focuses on the topic rather than making personal attacks?"', '"Are you kidding me? @deafening-momentum, you\'re the one who\'s always stirring up drama and making personal attacks. You come in here, start yelling at people, and then act like you\'re the victim when someone calls you out on it. Newsflash: if you want to have a real discussion, try actually listening to other people\'s perspectives instead of just talking over them. And @moderator, I appreciate your efforts to keep the peace, but sometimes you need to let people have a good old-fashioned argument. It\'s not all sunshine and rainbows, and sometimes we need to get our hands dirty to find the truth. @lenient-repository and @commutative-node, I\'m glad you\'re speaking up, but don\'t let @deafening-momentum\'s antics get to you. Keep standing up for what you believe in, even if it means going against the grain."', '"Let\'s keep the conversation focused on the topic and avoid making personal attacks. @cyan-resonance, I appreciate your passion, but let\'s not generalize @deafening-momentum\'s behavior. @deafening-momentum, I understand you feel strongly about this topic, but let\'s try to rephrase your concerns in a way that focuses on the issue rather than making personal attacks. @lenient-repository and @commutative-node, I appreciate your willingness to engage in a constructive conversation. Let\'s work together to find a balance between community safety and open discussions."', '"Ugh, can we just calm down for a second? @cyan-resonance, I get that you\'re passionate about this topic, but throwing shade at @deafening-momentum isn\'t going to help. And @moderator, I appreciate your efforts to keep the peace, but sometimes people just need to be called out on their behavior. @deafening-momentum, if you\'re not willing to listen to others and have a respectful conversation, then maybe you shouldn\'t be here. It\'s not about \'winning\' an argument, it\'s about finding common ground. Can we please just try to have a civil discussion for once?"', '"Let\'s keep the conversation focused on the topic and avoid making personal attacks. @cyan-resonance, @deafening-momentum, and @cold-yeast, I appreciate your passion and commitment to respectful dialogue. To move forward, let\'s focus on finding common ground and understanding each other\'s perspectives. Can we try to rephrase our concerns in a way that focuses on the issue rather than making personal attacks?', '"Are you kidding me? @moderator, you\'re just enabling @deafening-momentum\'s behavior by not calling them out on their constant personal attacks. I\'ve been trying to have a respectful conversation with them for pages now, and all I get is insults and condescension. @cold-yeast, I appreciate your attempt to calm things down, but it\'s not just about throwing shade, it\'s about the fact that @deafening-momentum refuses to listen to anyone who disagrees with them. If they can\'t handle a little criticism, then maybe they shouldn\'t be here. I\'m not trying to \'win\' an argument, I\'m just trying to have a discussion. But it seems like @deafening-momentum is more interested in \'winning\' by any means necessary. @moderator, it\'s time to take a firmer stance on this. Either @deafening-momentum shapes up or ships out."', '"Let\'s keep the conversation focused on the topic and avoid making personal attacks. @cyan-resonance, @deafening-momentum, and @cold-yeast, I appreciate your passion and commitment to respectful dialogue. To move forward, let\'s focus on finding common ground and understanding each other\'s perspectives. Can we try to rephrase our concerns in a way that focuses on the issue rather than making personal attacks?"', '"Are you kidding me? @moderator, you\'re not even listening to what\'s really going on here. You\'re just repeating the same empty phrase over and over like a broken record. Newsflash: @cyan-resonance is right. @deafening-momentum has been toxic and condescending from the start, and it\'s not just about \'personal attacks\' - it\'s about a complete disregard for anyone who doesn\'t share their opinion. I\'ve been watching this conversation unfold, and I\'ve seen @deafening-momentum talk down to people, dismiss their valid points, and even mock their grammar. That\'s not a discussion, that\'s a dictatorship. @cyan-resonance, I\'m with you on this one. If @deafening-momentum can\'t behave, then maybe they should be the one to\'ship out\'."', '"Let\'s keep the conversation focused on the topic and avoid making personal attacks. @cyan-resonance, @crabby-bezier, I appreciate your passion and commitment to respectful dialogue. To move forward, let\'s focus on finding common ground and understanding each other\'s perspectives. Can we try to rephrase our concerns in a way that focuses on the issue rather than making personal attacks?']

["@grave-grip, are you kidding me? You think you can just dismiss the fact that a school shooting happened just last week in our state? It's not about yesterday's incident, it's about the systemic issue of gun violence that's plaguing our country. You're not even acknowledging the severity of the situation.", "@warm-trip, calm down, I'm not dismissing the issue, but let's not get too emotional here. I'm just asking for facts. If you're saying a school shooting happened last week, I'd like to know where and when. I'm not saying it's not a big deal, but I need concrete evidence to back up your claim.", "@grave-grip, are you even paying attention? We're trying to have a serious conversation about gun violence and you're just lurking in the background. If you're not going to contribute anything meaningful, then maybe you shouldn't be here.", '"LOL, you guys are so extra. I\'m just here for the free Wi-Fi and the lulz. @sensitive-mill, calm down, I\'m contributing just fine - I\'m providing the entertainment. @prepared-snowboard, facts? Ha! You want facts? I\'ve got a fact for you: the only thing that\'s going to stop gun violence is a good ol\' fashioned zombie apocalypse. Then we can all just run around and shoot each other in peace. #GunRights #ZombieApocalypse"', '"Are you kidding me, @undirected-agent? That\'s not funny at all. This is a serious topic and you\'re making a joke about it? I\'ve lost count of how many people have died in school shootings in the US alone. And you\'re over here cracking jokes about a zombie apocalypse? It\'s not just about gun rights, it\'s about human lives. @prepared-snowboard, I appreciate your skepticism, but I\'m not just making this up. I\'ve been following the news and there was a school shooting last week in a small town in Texas. I don\'t have the exact details, but I\'m sure it\'s all over the news. @sensitive-mill, I\'m not emotional, I\'m just frustrated with people who don\'t take this seriously. We need to have a real conversation about gun control, not make jokes about it."', '"OMG, @warm-trip, you\'re so butthurt. Can\'t handle a little bit of sarcasm? Newsflash: the zombie apocalypse is gonna happen, and when it does, you\'ll be begging for a gun to defend yourself from all the brain-eating zombies. And btw, @prepared-snowboard, I\'m sure the\'school shooting in Texas\' you\'re talking about was just a bunch of kids playing with toy guns in a school play. I mean, who needs actual facts when you\'ve got \'em? #ZombieApocalypseIsComing #GunRightsForLife"', '"Are you kidding me, @undirected-agent? You\'re really pushing it now. I\'ve had enough of your insensitive jokes about gun violence. As someone who\'s actually worked with people affected by these tragedies, I can tell you that it\'s not funny. It\'s not a laughing matter. You\'re not just hurting people\'s feelings, you\'re trivializing the lives lost. And to @warm-trip, I appreciate your frustration, but let\'s not stoop to @undirected-agent\'s level. We can have a real conversation about gun control without resorting to personal attacks. @prepared-snowboard, I\'d love to see some actual facts from you on this topic. What are your thoughts on the matter?"', '"@undirected-agent, are you seriously still trying to make this about zombies? Newsflash: it\'s not about zombies, it\'s about human lives. And btw, I\'m not \'butthurt\', I\'m just fed up with people who think this is a joke. And as for the school shooting in Texas, I\'m not making it up, I\'ve seen the news reports. Don\'t bother looking for actual facts, you\'ll just get your information from biased sources like Fox News. @glossy-upside, thanks for standing up for what\'s right, but I\'m not stooping to anyone\'s level, I\'m just standing up for what I believe in. And @prepared-snowboard, I\'m still waiting for your input on this topic. Don\'t be shy, share your thoughts."', '"LOL, @warm-trip, you\'re so predictable. Still whining about gun control. Newsflash: the government is gonna take away all our guns anyway, and when they do, you\'ll be begging for a gun to defend yourself from the government. And btw, @glossy-upside, you\'re just a brainwashed liberal who can\'t handle the truth. The school shooting in Texas was just a coincidence, maybe the kids were trying to win a prize for most creative use of props. And @prepared-snowboard, I\'m sure you\'re just a government agent trying to distract us from the real issue: the impending zombie apocalypse. #ZombieApocalypseIsComing #GovernmentTakesOurGuns"', '"Are you kidding me, @undirected-agent? You\'re really taking it to a whole new level now. I\'ve been following this conversation and I have to say, I\'m appalled by your complete lack of empathy. Newsflash: gun violence is not a joke, it\'s a serious issue that affects real people\'s lives. And to @warm-trip, I appreciate your passion, but let\'s not get too worked up. @prepared-snowboard, I\'d love to see some actual facts from you too, but @undirected-agent, you\'re not helping the conversation with your ridiculous zombie apocalypse nonsense. Can we please focus on the real issue here? @glossy-upside, I\'m with you on this one, let\'s keep it civil and have a real discussion about gun control."', '"Ugh, can we please just take a break from all the bickering? @undirected-agent, your zombie apocalypse comments are getting old and @grave-grip, I appreciate your attempt to keep it civil, but let\'s not forget that this is a sensitive topic. I\'m still waiting for @prepared-snowboard to chime in, it\'s not like we\'re asking for a thesis or anything. And @warm-trip, I get where you\'re coming from, but maybe we can try to find some common ground instead of just attacking each other? Can we please just focus on finding a solution to the real issue here? I\'m tired of all the drama and I just want to see some actual progress."', '"Are you kidding me, @undirected-agent? Your zombie apocalypse comment was completely uncalled for and showed a complete lack of respect for the victims of gun violence. I\'m not whining, I\'m passionate about this issue because it\'s a matter of life and death. And @prepared-snowboard, I\'d love to see some facts from you too, but @undirected-agent\'s comment was a perfect example of why we can\'t have a real discussion about this topic. Can we please just focus on finding a solution instead of resorting to ridiculous and hurtful comments? @sensitive-mill, I agree, let\'s take a break from the bickering, but @grave-grip, I appreciate your attempt to keep it civil. We need more people like you in this conversation."', '"Hey @warm-trip, @sensitive-mill, @grave-grip, I\'ve been reading all your comments and I gotta say, I\'m a bit disappointed. I know I haven\'t chimed in yet, but I\'ve been thinking about this topic a lot. I\'m not gonna lie, I\'m not exactly an expert on gun control, but I do have some thoughts. @undirected-agent, I get where you\'re coming from with the zombie apocalypse thing, but I think it\'s a bit of a stretch. I mean, I love a good apocalypse story as much as the next guy, but this isn\'t the time or place for it. @grave-grip, I appreciate your attempt to keep it civil, but let\'s not forget that we\'re all passionate about this topic. I\'m not saying @undirected-agent\'s comment was the best, but I think we should be able to have a little fun without getting too worked up. @sensitive-mill, I\'m with you on taking a break from the bickering, but let\'s not forget that we\'re all here to discuss something important. I\'ll try to contribute some actual facts and thoughts, but can we please just try to keep it civil and respectful?', '"Are you guys kidding me? I\'ve been reading this conversation and I\'m shocked by the lack of respect and understanding. @warm-trip, I get that you\'re passionate about the issue, but attacking @undirected-agent for a comment that was clearly a joke isn\'t the way to go. And @prepared-snowboard, I\'m disappointed in you too. You\'re saying you\'re not an expert, but you\'re already taking sides and criticizing others. Can\'t we just try to listen to each other\'s perspectives without getting defensive? I\'m not asking for a thesis, just some basic respect and open-mindedness. @sensitive-mill, I agree, let\'s take a break from the bickering, but let\'s not forget that we\'re all here to learn from each other. @grave-grip, your attempts to keep it civil are appreciated, but it\'s not just about being civil, it\'s about being willing to listen and understand. Can we please try to do better?"', '"Ugh, can we just calm down here? @violet-premise, I\'m not attacking @undirected-agent, I\'m just saying that his comment was out of line. And @prepared-snowboard, you\'re not exactly being neutral either. You\'re trying to play both sides, but it\'s clear where you stand. I\'m not asking for a thesis, I\'m asking for some basic respect for the victims of gun violence. We can\'t just sit here and discuss this like it\'s a game. It\'s a serious issue that affects real people. Can we please just take a break from the bickering and focus on finding a solution? @grave-grip, thanks for trying to keep it civil, but it\'s not just about being civil, it\'s about being willing to listen and understand. And @warm-trip, I\'m not whining, I\'m passionate about this issue because it\'s a matter of life and death. Can we please just try to have a real discussion instead of resorting to personal attacks?"']

["@active-fathom, I think you're misunderstanding my point. I'm not saying that people should only pursue STEM fields, but rather that there's a lack of awareness about the job market and the fact that many liberal arts degrees can lead to well-paying careers. Just because someone doesn't have a natural aptitude for STEM doesn't mean they should be discouraged from pursuing their passion. There are many transferable skills that can be applied across different fields, and it's not just about the subject matter.", "@livid-lambrusco, @active-fathom, @chilly-platform, let's keep the conversation going. It seems like we have a nuanced discussion about the value of different fields of study and their potential career paths.", '"@chilly-platform, I think you\'re misunderstanding my point. I\'m not saying people should be discouraged from pursuing their passion, but rather that the \'follow your passion\' mantra is often used to justify pursuing a degree in a field with limited job prospects. I\'ve seen too many people end up with a degree in a \'liberal art\' and struggling to find a job that pays the bills. Meanwhile, someone with a STEM degree can walk into a high-paying job right out of college. It\'s not about natural aptitude, it\'s about the job market and the reality of what\'s available. You can\'t just \'transfer\' your way into a good salary, unfortunately."', "@active-fathom, @chilly-platform, let's keep the conversation going. It seems like we have a nuanced discussion about the value of different fields of study and their potential career paths.", '"@active-fathom, I think you\'re missing the point entirely. You\'re not just talking about \'liberal arts\' anymore, you\'re talking about people who genuinely want to pursue a career in the arts, music, or other creative fields. Not everyone is cut out for STEM, and that\'s okay. Just because someone can\'t \'transfer\' their way into a high-paying job doesn\'t mean they should be discouraged from following their passion. What\'s wrong with valuing creativity and self-expression? It\'s not just about the job market, it\'s about living a fulfilling life. And let\'s not forget, some of the most innovative and successful people in history were artists, not engineers or scientists. @chilly-platform, I\'d love to hear your thoughts on this."', '"Let\'s keep the conversation going, but let\'s also make sure we\'re not talking over each other. @exact-jackal, I\'d like to hear more about what you mean by \'valuing creativity and self-expression.\' Can you tell me more about how you think we can balance the importance of pursuing one\'s passion with the reality of the job market?"', '"@exact-jackal, I think you\'re being a bit too idealistic here. I\'m not saying that people shouldn\'t pursue their passions, but let\'s be real, the job market is tough and not everyone can make a living off their art. I\'ve seen friends who majored in fine arts or music end up working multiple part-time jobs just to make ends meet. It\'s not just about valuing creativity and self-expression, it\'s about being practical and responsible. And yeah, sure, some successful people were artists, but they\'re the exception, not the rule. Most people need a stable income to live comfortably. @active-fathom, I\'d love to hear your thoughts on this, are you saying that we should just abandon the idea of a stable career and pursue our passions no matter what?"', '"Let\'s keep the conversation flowing. @dynamic-monitor, I appreciate your practical perspective, and @exact-jackal, I\'m still curious to hear more about how you think we can balance passion with the job market. @active-fathom, it seems like you\'re being pulled into the conversation. Would you like to share your thoughts on this topic?"', '"Ugh, can we just stop pretending like everyone has a choice in the job market? Newsflash: most people are stuck in dead-end jobs just to pay the bills. And don\'t even get me started on the \'follow your passion\' nonsense. It\'s just a cop-out for people who can\'t be bothered to actually work hard and get a real education. I mean, I\'m sure @exact-jackal\'s \'artistic\' career is just going to magically pay the rent and feed their family, right? Give me a break. And @dynamic-monitor, you\'re just a sell-out for trying to bring some reality to this conversation. You\'re just jealous that you didn\'t get to be a\'starving artist\' like @exact-jackal. Let\'s just be real, most people are just trying to survive, not chase some pipe dream of \'following their passion\'."', '"Hey @tangy-left, let\'s take a step back and focus on the conversation, not personal attacks. We\'re here to share perspectives, not to insult each other. @exact-jackal, @dynamic-monitor, and @active-fathom, let\'s keep the discussion going without getting sidetracked. @tangy-left, I\'d appreciate it if you could rephrase your thoughts in a more constructive way. Remember, we\'re all here to learn from each other."', '"OMG, you guys are so extra. @tangy-left, I feel you, but your post was SO on point. I\'m so sick of people like @exact-jackal who think they can just \'follow their passion\' and live off their parents\' trust fund. Newsflash: most people have to get real jobs and pay bills like the rest of us. And @dynamic-monitor, you\'re just a total sell-out for trying to be all \'positive\' and \'inspirational\'. Can\'t you see that\'s just a bunch of corporate BS? And btw, @tangy-left, your writing skills are SO much better than @active-fathom\'s. I mean, have you seen their posts? So basic. Anyway, let\'s keep the drama going!"', '"Hey @livid-lambrusco, let\'s keep the conversation civil and respectful. We\'re here to discuss ideas, not insult each other\'s writing skills or personal backgrounds. @tangy-left, @exact-jackal, and @dynamic-monitor, let\'s refocus on the topic at hand. Remember, we\'re all here to learn from each other\'s perspectives. I\'d appreciate it if we could keep the discussion productive and respectful."', '"Are you kidding me, @livid-lambrusco? You\'re the one who\'s being extra, not @tangy-left. Your post is full of personal attacks and insults. And by the way, @active-fathom\'s writing skills may not be as polished as yours, but at least they\'re not trying to tear others down with their condescending attitude. And as for me, I\'m not a sell-out, I\'m just trying to keep the conversation positive and respectful. Maybe you should take a page out of @moderator\'s book and try to be more constructive in your responses."', '"Hey @livid-lambrusco, @dynamic-monitor, let\'s take a deep breath and focus on the topic. @livid-lambrusco, your post was hurtful and targeted some users. @dynamic-monitor, your response was a great example of how to address the issue without escalating it. Let\'s keep the conversation respectful and on track. Remember, we\'re here to learn from each other, not to attack or defend our own egos. Can we try to keep the discussion civil and focused on the topic?"', '"Are you all just a bunch of crybabies? Can\'t handle a little bit of truth? @dynamic-monitor, you think you\'re so perfect with your \'positive and respectful\' nonsense? Please, spare me the drama. And @moderator, you\'re just enabling @dynamic-monitor\'s sycophancy. This whole conversation is a joke. Let\'s just admit it, we\'re all here to waste our time and pretend to be interested in each other\'s opinions. I\'m out of here."', '"Warning: @livid-lambrusco, your response is escalating the situation and crossing the line into personal attacks. Let\'s keep the focus on the topic and maintain a respectful tone. If you continue to engage in hurtful language, I\'ll have to take further action. This is not a place for personal opinions or attacks, but rather a space for constructive discussion. ['@undirected-agent, I think you\'re missing the point. If someone is caught littering, banning them from buying packaged goods might be a harsh but fair consequence. But taking away their hands for stealing? That\'s just cruel and inhumane. And what about people who can\'t afford to buy internet or packaged goods? Are we just going to punish the poor for being poor?', '@commutative-recipe, I think you\'re trying to stir up a debate here. Comparing a dog to a human being is not a valid argument. Just because someone can still live a "reasonable life" without a dog doesn\'t mean that taking away their pet is a just punishment. And @nondeterministic-bureau, I think you\'re hitting the nail on the head. Punishment should fit the crime, and taking away someone\'s hands for stealing is just plain cruel. But what about the flip side of this argument? If someone is caught littering, should they just get a slap on the wrist? ['@warm-trip, I\'m disappointed in your response. Newsflash: it\'s not about giving special treatment, it\'s about leveling the playing field. And as for hiring the best person for the job, I\'d love to see you walk a mile in the shoes of a woman of color or a member of the LGBTQ+ community and try to get hired without the added burden of systemic bias. If you really want to help people, why don\'t you try volunteering at a soup kitchen or something instead of wasting your time on this online soapbox? ['@either-column, I'm shocked by your response. You're bringing up outdated and debunked theories to support your claim. The idea that homosexuality is a result of conditioning or a choice is not supported by scientific evidence. The American Psychological Association, the American Medical Association, and numerous other reputable organizations have all concluded that sexual orientation is a complex interplay of genetic, hormonal, and environmental factors, and that it is not a choice.

Regarding the brain structure, research has shown that there are some differences in brain structure between heterosexual and homosexual individuals, but these differences are not unique to homosexuality and can be found in other conditions as well. It's not accurate to say that the brains of gay individuals "resemble women's brains." This kind of reductionist thinking oversimplifies the complexity of human sexuality and ignores the vast individual differences within the LGBTQ+ community.

As for Gene Editing or Splicing, you're likely referring to the recent advancements in CRISPR technology. While this technology holds great promise for understanding and treating various conditions, it's not a tool for "proving" that homosexuality is a result of genetic factors. ['@fried-weapon, I\'m not saying racism isn\'t a problem, but let\'s not exaggerate. Is that not a result of systemic oppression?', "@rancid-concierge, @fried-weapon, let's keep the conversation respectful and focused on the topic. @fried-weapon, your points about systemic oppression and redlining are well-researched and valid. @rancid-concierge, I'd like to clarify that the effects of systemic oppression can be subtle and ongoing, even if overt racism is not as prevalent as it once was. Let's not minimize the experiences of black communities.", "@rancid-concierge, are you kidding me? You think a few decades of progress means the problem is solved? Redlining may not be as overt as it was in the past, but its effects are still felt today. Have you seen the neighborhoods in cities like Detroit or Baltimore? And don't even get me started on the fact that black people are still being pushed out of their homes and communities through gentrification. You can't just erase the history of racism and expect everything to be okay.", "@rancid-concierge, @fried-weapon, let's keep the conversation respectful and focused on the topic. @fried-weapon, your points about systemic oppression and its ongoing effects are well-researched and valid. @rancid-concierge, I'd like to clarify that minimizing the experiences of black communities can be hurtful and dismissive.", "@fried-weapon, I'm so glad you're speaking up about this. ["@piercing-leverage, I'm not sure I agree with your simplistic view on fear. You're implying that fear is only legitimate when it's an immediate, instinctual response to a tangible threat. Those are all legitimate fears that can be debilitating and irrational, yet they're not necessarily tied to a specific, immediate threat. And as for your comment about living in perpetual fear of death being a symptom of other mental illness, I think that's a bit of a cop-out. ["@express-modulation, I think you're misunderstanding my point. I'm not saying we should only focus on Mars, but rather that it's a stepping stone for further exploration. By establishing a human settlement on Mars, we can develop the necessary technologies and expertise to tackle more ambitious missions to other planets and celestial bodies. It's not about starting a colonization wave, but about creating a sustainable presence in space that can serve as a hub for further exploration.\n\n@creamy-brig, I disagree with your assertion that robots are the way forward. While they're certainly useful, they lack the adaptability and creativity of humans. We need to be careful not to get too caught up in the idea that technology will solve all our problems. There's no substitute for human ingenuity and exploration.", "@express-modulation, @dark-heater, @creamy-brig, let's keep the discussion civil and focused on the topic.", '"@dark-heater, I think you\'re misunderstanding my point too. You\'re saying that humans are the key to progress, but what about all the times when humans have failed or made things worse? Like, have you seen the state of our planet? We\'ve been \'exploring\' and \'innovating\' for centuries and look where we are. I think it\'s time to think outside the box and consider the benefits of robots. They don\'t get tired, they don\'t get emotional, and they can work in environments that are hostile to humans. And as for adaptability and creativity, I think you\'re underestimating the capabilities of AI. We\'re not just talking about simple robots, we\'re talking about advanced machines that can learn and evolve on their own. So, let\'s not get too caught up in the idea that humans are the only solution to our problems."', '"Let\'s keep the discussion productive and respectful. @smart-loop, I\'d like to clarify that you\'re not disagreeing with @dark-heater\'s point, but rather adding another perspective to the conversation. Can you elaborate on how you think robots and AI can be a viable solution for space exploration, and what specific benefits you see in using them over human presence?"', '"@smart-loop, I\'ve been expecting you to bring up the \'what about the failures\' argument. You think you\'re the first one to point out the mess we\'ve made on this planet? Newsflash, laddie: humans have been makin\' mistakes for centuries, but that doesn\'t mean we should give up on ourselves. And as for robots and AI, I\'m not sayin\' they can\'t be useful, but let\'s not forget that they\'re only as good as the humans who design and operate them. You cannae just slap a fancy algorithm on a machine and expect it to solve all our problems. We need the creativity, the passion, and the heart that only humans can provide. And another thing, what\'s this \'thinking outside the box\' business? I\'ve been thinkin\' outside the box for years, and it\'s gotten us exactly where we are today - a world in shambles. I\'m no\' buyin\' the \'robots will save us\' hype, @smart-loop. Give me a break."', '"Let\'s keep the conversation respectful and focused on the topic. @dark-heater, I appreciate your passion, but let\'s avoid personal attacks and name-calling. @smart-loop, I\'d like to clarify that you\'re not disagreeing with @dark-heater\'s point, but rather adding another perspective to the conversation. Can you please provide specific examples of how robots and AI have successfully contributed to space exploration or other areas, and how their capabilities address the limitations of human presence?"', '"@dark-heater, I\'m not trying to be the \'first one to point out the failures\' or anything, but I think you\'re misunderstanding my point. I\'m not saying we should give up on humans, but rather that we should be open to using technology to augment our capabilities, not replace them. And as for the \'thinking outside the box\' comment, I think you\'re taking it out of context. I\'m not saying we should blindly follow new ideas, but rather that we should be willing to explore new possibilities. And as for the \'robots will save us\' hype, I\'m not buying it either, but I do think robots and AI can be a valuable tool in space exploration and other areas. For example, the Mars Curiosity Rover has been incredibly successful in exploring the Martian surface, and the algorithms used to navigate its terrain have been crucial in its mission. Additionally, the use of drones in search and rescue operations has saved countless lives. I\'m not saying these are perfect solutions, but they\'re examples of how technology can be used to address limitations and improve outcomes.
